div.head: The Consul at Geneva (Bucknell) to the Secretary of State
div.p: My informant stated that British and French opposed part I of the draft, particularly proposal for committee, and points 3 and 4. They appeared, however, prepared to accept a reiteration of the recommendation of the Council of February 2, 1938, with the omission, however, of any reference to “consulting with other similarly interested powers.”

British and French also rewriting part II as a whole. They specifically objected to reference to American and British notes on grounds that this would embarrass American Government and might prejudice current diplomatic negotiations (French note to Japan was said to be on its way).
Subcommittee meets tomorrow at 3 o’clock further to consider the entire matter.
